content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder ("TODO") with no visible location feature (Info.plist:8-11; camera used in App/ScannerView.swift:13).
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings don't tell users why access is needed for a specific feature. The camera string should mention document scanning (the actual feature in ScannerView.swift), and the location string is a literal "TODO" with no location API usage found in the code — either remove the key or add a real feature-tied explanation. Fix both before submission, as vague or placeholder purpose strings are a common 5.1.1 rejection.
